channel.title: Latest alerts from washingtonpost.com
channel.description: An unofficial feed created by Derek Willis.

item.title: Former ICE deputy director Madison Sheahan loses GOP congressional primary in Ohio
item.description: Sheahan left her job as deputy director at U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement in January, hoping that her service in support of President Donald Trump’s top domestic policy issue would propel her to the nomination in Ohio’s 9th District. She was in third among five GOP candidates, with more than half the vote counted.

item.title: In a first, FDA authorizes fruit-flavored vapes for adults
item.description: The approval marks a shift after the FDA for years worked aggressively to regulate flavored vapes and came after President Donald Trump reportedly pressed the FDA commissioner to approve the product.

item.title: They thought their disabled daughter was safe. A pregnancy revealed her rape.
item.description: She’s blind and can barely speak, and her family is suing her Maryland group home, caregivers and state agencies. “The whole system failed Kamryn,” her mother says.

item.title: Activist who gave out fliers with Stephen Miller’s address won’t face charges
item.description: A prosecutor in Virginia cited “insufficient evidence” in the case involving the White House aide and said proceeding could have a “chilling effect on others wishing to engage in peaceful political protest.”

item.title: Chicago schools’ May Day field trip was wildly inappropriate
item.description: Sending students to political rallies undercuts parents' trust in teachers.

item.title: Federal agency sues New York Times, accusing it of bias against White male employee
item.description: The Equal Employment Opportunity Commission alleged that the newspaper violated civil rights law by denying a promotion to a White man and blamed its adherence to DEI policies.

item.title: Snubs and surprises from the Tony Award nominations
item.description: “Schmigadoon!,” “The Lost Boys” and “Ragtime” racked up the nods, while Lea Michele and Adrien Brody got snubbed.

item.title: Senate Republicans offer $1 billion for White House security, sparking dispute over Trump’s ballroom
item.description: Lawmakers maintain their proposal would authorize security construction, but not President Donald Trump’s ballroom. The White House disagrees.

item.title: Carolyn Hax: Empty nester bereft without ‘measured chaos’ of child-rearing
item.description: Introverted husband, friends and hobbies can’t give the extroverted letter writer enough to fill the next chapter of life.

item.title: Sen. Dick Durbin: Yes, Trump is deporting ‘dreamers.’ Now here’s the quiet part.
item.description: Processing holds. Delayed renewals. Much of the attack on DACA is hiding in plain sight.

item.title: The 9 best-dressed celebrities at the 2026 Met Gala
item.description: Attendees paid homage to artworks, to artists, to artistic concepts. Here are our favorite looks of the night.

item.title: A prominent lawyer in today’s On the Record
item.description: A prominent lawyer in today’s On the Record

item.title: Hegseth says U.S. mission to protect commercial ships in Strait of Hormuz is temporary; ceasefire with Iran remains in place
item.description: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth said the responsibility of protecting ships would be soon handed to other nations, emphasizing the fragile ceasefire remained in place despite attacks on U.S. ships on Monday.

item.title: Iran war. Midterms prep. White House lockdown. Abortion pills. Lurking microplastics. Cellphone bans. Met Gala. Catch up now.
item.description: Catch up in minutes with these 7 stories.

item.title: Celebrities hit the Met Gala's red carpet as protesters gathered to highlight wealth inequality. Follow our coverage.
item.description: This year’s annual event will christen the new Condé M. Nast Galleries at the Met. Protesters were outside the entrance to the gala, and New York Mayor Zohran Mamdani, a democratic socialist, has said he’s skipping it to maintain his focus on affordability.

item.title: A GOP lawmaker supported an immigration crackdown. Her husband paid a price.
item.description: The situation cost Idaho state Sen. Glenneda Zuiderveld and her husband most of their income and highlights an escalating split in the Republican Party.

item.title: Blake Lively and Justin Baldoni settle explosive lawsuit over ‘It Ends With Us’
item.description: The actors’ accusations against each other — including sexual harassment, retaliation and defamation — fueled months of online debate that quickly overshadowed the film.

item.title: Justice Department no longer plans to appeal court ruling that quashed Federal Reserve subpoenas
item.description: The development is the latest sign of surrender from U.S. Attorney Jeanine Pirro in the politically thorny probe of the independent central bank.

item.title: White House briefly locked down after person is shot by Secret Service officer
item.description: The shooting occurred a few blocks from the White House during a confrontation between an armed individual and Secret Service police, the agency said in a social media post. The agency said the person’s condition is unknown.

item.title: Washington Post wins public service Pulitzer for DOGE, federal workforce coverage
item.description: It’s the second time The Post has won the prestigious award in five years. Former staff photographer Jahi Chikwendiu also won the prize for feature photography.

item.title: White House lawyers prepare staff for possibility of dealing with a Democratic Congress after November midterms
item.description: The private briefings on best practices for handling congressional oversight come as polls indicate Democrats have a strong edge ahead of the midterm elections.

item.title: Iran fires on U.S. ships in Strait of Hormuz but does not land any hits, U.S. says. The attacks threaten the shaky ceasefire.
item.description: The attacks followed the passage of U.S. warships and merchant vessels through the waterway despite Iran’s threats against attempts to “interfere” in the strait.

item.title: Multiple D.C. police leaders face termination, at least some over an investigation into crime-data manipulation
item.description: Multiple high-ranking D.C. police officials have been served papers saying the department intends to fire them, according to three officials familiar with the situation.

item.title: Internal ICE records reveal widespread use of force in detention centers
item.description: The reports detail how guards have increasingly used chemical agents and physical tactics on detainees, including groups demanding adequate water, food and medical care.

item.title: Carolyn Hax: ‘I became my wife’s abuser.’ How does a person go on after that?
item.description: Wife left their marriage over the letter writer’s anger issues, but recovery and starting over are hard to envision.

item.title: Supreme Court allows patients to continue accessing abortion pill through the mail for now
item.description: The high court’s order reinstates mail access to the pill for one week while the justices consider emergency applications filed by drugmakers.

item.title: How microplastics are likely helping to heat up the planet
item.description: A new study indicates that minuscule pieces of plastic — particularly ones of various colors — are contributing to heating the atmosphere.

item.title: Millions of kids are missing out on a big financial gift
item.description: The process of opening Trump Accounts means lots of families fall through the cracks.

item.title: Outer Banks homes are collapsing into the ocean. Would this couple’s house be next?
item.description: A $50 million project to shore up a Buxton, North Carolina, beach where at least 19 homes have collapsed since September is set to start this month.

item.title: A new indictment in today’s On the Record
item.description: Answer now to earn your Monday points.

item.title: Strait of Hormuz. ICE detention. Rudy Giuliani. Abortion pills. Spirit Airlines. Vaping risk. Fun chores. Catch up now.
item.description: Catch up in minutes with these 7 stories.

item.title: 4 subtle signs you aren’t eating enough protein
item.description: The macronutrient is important for maintaining muscle as you age, but it’s possible you’re not getting as much as you think.

item.title: Trump says U.S. will guide ships through Strait of Hormuz
item.description: The president called the effort, which he said would begin Monday, a humanitarian gesture requested by other countries whose vessels, crews and supplies have been stranded for weeks.

item.title: The body’s most mysterious organ may play a key role in longevity and cancer
item.description: A raft of research is recasting the thymus from a bit player to a potent regulator of aging and immune health.

item.title: Trump hosts PGA Tour at Doral — and he’s the big winner
item.description: For a decade, golf’s biggest league shunned the president as his first presidential run unnerved sponsors. In his second term, it has welcomed him back.

item.title: Pirro says ballistic evidence shows correspondents’ dinner suspect shot at officer
item.description: The assertion by U.S. Attorney for D.C. Jeanine Pirro, made in an CNN interview, went further than what prosecutors have said in court filings and is the most detailed description yet of evidence supporting Justice Department officials’ public statements alleging the suspect fired his weapon at an officer.

item.title: He nearly joined Trump’s administration. Now he’s running for Congress as a Democrat.
item.description: Ten years ago, George Conway almost took a job with Donald Trump, then became one of his biggest critics. He lost his marriage but found a new political identity.

item.title: His wife's voice sustained this soldier for 177 days in a Russian 'kill zone'
item.description: While Roman Mongold was surrounded by the enemy for nearly six months, the Ukrainian soldier traded voice memos with his wife with a commander’s help.

item.title: The two swing states that could turn red under the Save America Act
item.description: Nationally, the GOP voting bill would probably be a wash — but not in New Mexico and Nevada.

item.title: Crossword and a coffee: Solve the Sunday puzzle now
item.description: Crossword and a coffee: Solve the Sunday puzzle now

item.title: Trump’s disapproval rating reaches new high, Post-ABC-Ipsos poll finds
item.description: Six months ahead of the midterm elections, Democrats now hold a five-point advantage in support for Congress, up from two points in February.

item.title: Doctors told her to remove her uterus. The real cause of her pain lay elsewhere.
item.description: After years of worsening pain, irregular periods and soaring hormone levels, doctors discovered it wasn’t her reproductive organs driving her illness.

item.title: Is Red Lobster’s revamped ‘Endless Shrimp’ deal worth it? Our food critic tried it.
item.description: The promotion that once helped sink the business has returned. Elazar Sontag ate 43 of the garlicky and fried delights.

item.title: Hegseth tightens control at Pentagon, defiant and more confident than ever
item.description: Trump’s combative defense secretary has grown more self-assured of his job security despite his early missteps in the role, people familiar with the matter said.

item.title: Trump fundraiser shares plans for proposed ‘Garden of Heroes,’ East Potomac Golf Course remake as takeover looms
item.description: A fundraising pitch obtained by The Washington Post touts plans, which have not been approved, to dramatically reshape parts of Washington’s waterfront.

item.title: Elon Musk went to court. The judge wasn’t amused.
item.description: Elon Musk’s outsize reputation, provocative remarks and his friction with a federal judge have dominated the trial in his case against ChatGPT-maker OpenAI.

item.title: No, Spirit won’t rebook your flight. What travelers need to know after the airline ceased operations.
item.description: Budget carrier Spirit, which shut down early Saturday morning, offered some answers on its website — and much of the news is bad for its customers.

item.title: This fragile document deserved a celebration. At 100, it got one.
item.description: How the Declaration of Independence became the hit of the 1876 Centennial in Philadelphia.

item.title: Residents of a small town, a mix of conservative and liberal, discuss what’s really ailing American politics
item.description: Ten people gathered for a discussion about the rural-urban divide. They came together on a surprising solution.

item.title: How many guesses will Keyword take you today? Try to beat the average now.
item.description: How many guesses will Keyword take you today? Try to beat the average now.
